title: WireViz harness
description:
Schema for WireViz YAML — describes connectors, cables, and how they connect.

See https://github.com/wireviz/WireViz/blob/master/docs/syntax.md for the full reference.

metadata.description: Free-form metadata. Common keys are description, title, author. Surfaces in the rendered HTML output.

bgcolor.description: Background color of the whole diagram. Default: WH (white).

color_mode.description: How wire colors are labelled. SHORT shows IEC 2-letter codes (default), FULL spells them out, HEX uses #rrggbb.

tweak.description: Raw GraphViz overrides applied after normal emission. Power-user escape hatch.

connectors.description: Map of designator → connector definition. Each key (e.g. X1, J1) is the connector's name as it appears in the diagram.

cables.description: Map of designator → cable definition. Each key (e.g. W1) is the cable's name as it appears in the diagram.

connections.description: List of connection sets. Each set is itself a list that alternates between connector references (e.g. {X1: [1,2,3]}) and cable/arrow references — pin counts on each side must match.

additional_bom_items.description: Extra BOM entries that don't appear in the diagram itself (labels, sleeves, hardware).

connector.description: A single connector (plug, socket, ferrule, ring terminal, etc.).

type.description: Connector family — e.g. "D-Sub", "Molex KK 254", "H4 sealed beam".

style.description: Set to "simple" for ferrules / single-pin terminals — they render as pills rather than tables.

pincount.description: Number of pins. Use this OR pinlabels — pincount auto-numbers them, pinlabels names them.

pinlabels.description: Named pins (e.g. [GND, VCC, RX, TX]). Implies pincount = length of this list.

pins.description: Custom pin identifiers if you want non-sequential numbering (e.g. [A1, A2, B1]).

notes.description: Free-form notes shown on the connector node.

additional_components.description: Extra parts associated with this connector (crimps, pins) — appear in the BOM.

loops.description: Pairs of pins to draw as loops back to the connector itself (jumpers).

cable.description: A cable, bundle, or single wire run.

category.description: Set to "bundle" for individually-coloured wires not bound by a common jacket.

wirecount.description: Number of conductors. Implied by colors / color_code if those are set.

length.description: Cable length. Pair with length_unit (default: m).

gauge.description: Wire gauge — accepts "0.25 mm2", "24 AWG", or a bare number with gauge_unit set.

show_equiv.description: Auto-calculate the AWG↔mm² equivalent next to the gauge.

color_code.description:
Standard color scheme — fills `colors` automatically.

- DIN 47100 (European, ~64 colors)
- IEC 60757 (10 colors)
- BW (black/white)
- TEL (25-pair POTS)
- TELALT (25-pair, alt order)
- T568A / T568B (Ethernet)

colors.description: Explicit per-wire colors (IEC 2-letter codes). Overrides color_code.

shield.description: true = unlabelled shield drain. A color code uses that as the shield color.

image.description: Inline image attached to a connector / cable node.

src.description: Relative or absolute path to the image.

additionalComponent.description: Extra BOM line attached to this part (crimps, pins, sleeves).

qty_multiplier.description: Auto-multiply qty by a derived count.

colorCode.description: IEC 60757 two-letter color code. Combine codes for multi-colour wires (e.g. WHGN = white/green stripe). Codes are case-sensitive — use uppercase. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IEC_60757 for the full set.
